name: Salmon Corncakes
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Finely diced red bell pepper
1/2 cup Nonfat plain yogurt, strained for 15 minutes
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. When poaching salmon, whether to serve on its own or to use as an ingredient, season your liquid well with white wine, peppercorns, celery leaves, fresh parsley, and a bay leaf for greatest flavor.
- Combine water, wine, bay leaf, peppercorns, parsley, and celery leaves in a shallow 8x8-inch pan. Slowly bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and add salmon steaks. Simmer until salmon is just cooked through (7-10 minutes, depending on thickness). Remove with a slotted spatula, drain, and cool slightly.
- Flake the salmon into a bowl; discard skin and bones.
2. Add corn, shallots, red pepper, celery, and cilantro to the flaked salmon. Gently fold together with a rubber spatula.
3. In a separate bowl, combine yogurt, mayonnaise, mustard, and Tabasco sauce. Fold into the salmon mixture. Season with salt and pepper.
- Gently fold in the egg and egg white, then add 1/4 cup of cracker crumbs to the salmon mixture.
4. Form the mixture into 8 large or 12 medium-sized patties. Place some cracker crumbs on a plate and coat the patties on both sides, using more crumbs as needed. Refrigerate, covered, for up to 1 hour.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Cook the salmon corncakes a few at a time until golden brown (about 3 minutes per side). Add more oil to the skillet as needed. Serve immediately with Festival Corn Salsa alongside.
- Serve 8 pieces as an entree or 12 pieces as appetizers.
- Per serving (based on 8 cakes) without salsa: 366 calories, 26 grams fat, 50 milligrams cholesterol.
